Phase-52.NAME: KrotPN Matrix Visual System Foundation
Phase-52.summary: Create the shared Matrix visual runtime and style foundation for KrotPN user and admin frontends, based on the approved TRUFFLEX reference effects.

references.reference-1: TRUFFLEX matrix-background.js: Matrix rain canvas, pointer influence, resize recomputation, reduced-motion static frame.
references.reference-2: TRUFFLEX styles.css: scanlines, neon flicker, glitch accents, matrix fade, focus states, and reduced-motion policy.

constraints.constraint-2: No backend API or auth behavior changes.
constraints.constraint-3: No TRUFFLEX music, NFT copy, spider physics, fluid cursor, or WebGL glow in this phase.
constraints.constraint-4: Every created source file must include MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, CHANGE_SUMMARY, and semantic block anchors.

step-1.action: Translate the approved TRUFFLEX visual requirements into KrotPN-specific runtime and CSS contracts.
step-1.acceptance: M-070 and M-071 contracts remain the source of truth before code execution starts.

step-2.action: Create React/Vite MatrixBackground runtime with canvas guard, resize recomputation, pointer influence, requestAnimationFrame lifecycle, and static reduced-motion frame.
step-2.acceptance: Runtime can mount, resize, stop, and unmount without console errors or click interception.

step-3.action: Create VisualShell mount points for user and admin layouts with safe fixed canvas and overlay z-index boundaries.
step-3.acceptance: User and admin content remains above the visual layer and all controls remain interactive.

step-4.action: Define Matrix design tokens, compact panel primitives, CRT scanlines, glitch/flicker accents, focus states, and reduced-motion CSS policy.
step-4.acceptance: CSS remains compact, readable, and aligned with M-038 mobile-first operational rules.

step-5.action: Add smoke verification for canvas nonblank, reduced-motion fallback, pointer-events safety, no console errors, and no deploy/install diff.
step-5.acceptance: scripts/phase52-matrix-visual-smoke.mjs is ready for execute phase verification.

step-6.action: Wire the visual foundation into both frontends without changing route structures, auth state, API clients, or business workflows.
step-6.acceptance: Frontend and admin frontend builds pass locally.

step-7.action: Run desktop and mobile screenshot checks for canvas visibility, overlay readability, no horizontal scroll, and no text overlap.

step-8.action: Update module contracts, verification evidence, MyGRACE indexes, and current-status once implementation evidence exists.
step-8.acceptance: MyGRACE lint and XML parse pass after execution.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: User opens /login: VisualShell mounts MatrixBackground behind the login form, form focus remains visible, and reduced-motion users receive a static readable frame.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Admin opens /mtproto: operational tables and actions remain compact above the canvas, no overlay intercepts click or hover actions.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Browser lacks canvas context: Matrix runtime logs a controlled marker and the app continues with a static dark Matrix surface.

verification.entry-1: V-M-070 verifies Matrix runtime lifecycle, nonblank canvas, reduced-motion fallback, input pass-through, and runtime observability.
verification.entry-2: V-M-071 verifies Matrix style tokens, compact mobile layout, readable CRT/glitch effects, focus states, and protected-surface isolation.
verification.phase-gate: Phase-52 execute may start after both verification entries are status verification-designed and MyGRACE lint passes.
